claim_text: Katyayani Peeth is a current temple-ashram complex in Vrindavan represented by Katyayani Trust.
The institution identifies Swami Keshavanand as connected to Lahiri Mahasaya and as consecrator of the Vrindavan Katyayani temple in 1923.
notes: Own-lineage claim; not independent historical proof.

claim_text: Fysieke plek bevestigd; actuele bezoekbaarheid niet betrouwbaar vastgesteld.
notes: No reliable current access rules retrieved.

claim_text: A Neem Karoli Baba ashram complex exists in Vrindavan and includes his samadhi.
The complex is a physical anchor of the Neem Karoli Baba devotional lineage.
Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi is an active temple complex in Mathura.
The exact present managing institution was not established and must not be conflated with similarly named litigation trusts.
Radha Kund is a distinct named sacred water body adjoining Shyam Kund near Govardhan.
Shyam Kund is a distinct named sacred water body adjoining Radha Kund.
Govardhan Hill is a physical landscape object distinct from the Govardhan Parikrama route.
Govardhan Parikrama is a physically traceable living circumambulation practice around Govardhan.
notes: Government Braj body recognizes parikrama as pilgrimage practice.

claim_text: Kusum Sarovar is a current named heritage object in Govardhan.
Specific current living practice at Kusum Sarovar was not reliably established in this sweep.
